title: Optional service-worker offline cache + content-version manifest/hard-invalidation
context: The primary offline distribution is the versioned print bundle. An optional service worker (SW) can additionally cache the web kit for offline use in browsers. However, SW caching of safety-critical content (especially the electrical triage notes) carries a stale-content risk: a corrected electrical safety note must be immediately delivered, not served from a stale cache. The SW must implement a content-version manifest and hard-invalidation mechanism so that corrected safety notes are purged from the cache and re-fetched non-dismissibly. This feature depends on the offline ADR selected in arch-001.
objective: Implement an optional service worker that caches the web kit for offline use, with a content-version manifest and hard-invalidation mechanism that ensures corrected safety-critical notes (especially electrical triage content) are immediately re-fetched and never served stale.
acceptanceCriteria: The service worker is optional/progressive: the site functions fully without it (the versioned print bundle remains the primary offline path)
A content-version manifest lists each cached content unit with a version hash; when a unit's hash changes (e.g. a corrected electrical safety note), the SW hard-invalidates the stale cached version
The hard-invalidation is non-dismissible: when a cached safety-critical unit has a newer version, the SW forces a re-fetch and updates the cache before serving the new content
The SW introduces no telemetry, no PII, and no unexpected outbound network requests; the runtime network-interception E2E test passes with the SW active
The SW is correctly registered and unregistered; the CI offline E2E test passes with the SW both present and absent; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: A merged PR adding an optional service worker with content-version manifest and hard-invalidation for safety-critical content, passing CI no-telemetry/PII and offline E2E tests.
